---
title: "transform_mean"
generated: true
description: "Fréchet (Karcher) mean of homogeneous transforms (rigid or affine)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.geometry.algebra`](../nitrix-geometry-algebra.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:transform_mean -->
```python
transform_mean(transforms: Float[Array, 'k d1 d1'], *, weights: Optional[Float[Array, ' k']] = None, iters: int = 10, return_residual: bool = False) -> Float[Array, 'd1 d1'] | tuple[Float[Array, 'd1 d1'], Float[Array, '']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:transform_mean -->
Fréchet (Karcher) mean of homogeneous transforms (rigid or affine).

The Riemannian barycentre, found by the standard log/exp fixed point
$\mu \leftarrow \mu \cdot \exp(\sum_k w_k \cdot \log(\mu^{-1} T_k))$
(`iters` steps started from `transforms[0]`) in the true matrix chart.
The mean of rigid transforms is rigid; of affines, affine.  The Lie-algebra
mean is a plain weighted sum, and the matrix log is smooth at identity
(unlike the $\mathfrak{so}(n)$ rotation-vector log), so the iteration
converges from the first-element initialisation.

`iters` is a *hard cap*, not a tolerance loop: a data-dependent
`float(residual) < tol` break would forfeit the jit- and grad-cleanliness
this op advertises (the Python loop unrolls under trace; a host-synced break
does not).  It converges in a few steps for a *clustered* cohort, but a
**widely dispersed** one (rotation spread $\gtrsim \pi/2$) may not
converge within `iters` -- raise `iters` and use `return_residual` to
*check* rather than trust convergence.  A near-**antipodal** cohort (a pair
$\approx \pi$ apart) lies on the negative-real-axis boundary of
`matrix_log`: the tangent is NaN there, which propagates to a NaN mean
(a loud "the mean is ill-defined", not silent garbage).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:transform_mean -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `transforms` | `Float` | `k` × `d1` × `d1` | — | `(K, ndim+1, ndim+1)` stack of homogeneous transforms (rigid or affine; the linear blocks need positive determinant -- the [`rigid_exp`](../nitrix-geometry-transform/rigid_exp.qmd) / [`affine_exp`](../nitrix-geometry-transform/affine_exp.qmd) regime). |
| `weights` | `Optional[Float[Array, ' k']]` | — | `None` | Optional `(K,)` array of non-negative weights (normalised internally to sum to one); `None` gives uniform weights. |
| `iters` | `int` | — | `10` | Karcher fixed-point iteration **cap** (converges fast for clustered inputs; a dispersed cohort may need more -- check `return_residual`). |
| `return_residual` | `bool` | — | `False` | If `True`, also return the scalar Karcher residual (see Returns). |

**Returns** `Float` — shape —. The mean transform, of shape `(ndim+1, ndim+1)`, when `return_residual=False`. When `return_residual=True`, the pair `(mean, residual)`, where `residual` is the scalar norm of the final update tangent $\|\sum_k w_k \log(\mu^{-1} T_k)\|$ -- which tends to zero at a converged barycentre. A non-small (or NaN) residual flags a non-converged or ill-defined mean, so the caller can assert convergence rather than trust it.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:transform_mean -->
::: {.callout-note}
Uses `matrix_log` (hence `safe_inv`): jit- and grad-clean on a
healthy GPU; a forward / eager op on a machine with a wedged cuSOLVER.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:transform_mean -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `k` | kernel or basis components |
| `d1` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
